text:
Associated Press

Published:July 30, 2024 at 5:57 AM

Updated:July 30, 2024 at 2:34 PM

Associated Press

PARISвАУ A storm of outrage aboutthe Paris OlympicsвАЩopening ceremony вАФ including angry comments from Donald Trump вАФ took a legal turn Tuesday, with French prosecutors ordering police to investigate complaints of online abuse from a DJ and LGBTQ+ icon who performed.

DJ Barbara Butch said she suffered a torrent of online threats in the wake of a contentious scene at the Games' opening ceremony. A lawyer for Butch told The Associated Press that she had filed a formal legal complaint alleging online harassment, death threats, and insults. The lawyer, Audrey Msellati, said the complaint doesnвАЩt name any specific perpetrator or perpetrators of the alleged crimes.

Recommended Videos

The Paris prosecutorвАЩs office confirmed that it received ButchвАЩs complaint and said it tasked a police unit that specializes in fighting hate crimes to investigate. The police probe will focus on вАЬdiscriminatory messages based on religion or sexual orientation that were sent to her or posted online,вАЭ it said.

Although the ceremony'sartistic director Thomas Jollyhas repeatedly said that he wasn't inspired by вАЬThe Last Supper,вАЭcritics interpreted part of the showthat featured Butch as a mockery of Leonardo Da Vinci's painting showing Jesus Christ and his apostles. Butch, who calls herself a вАЬlove activist,вАЭ wore a silver headdress that looked like a halo as she got a party going during her segment of the show. Drag artists, dancers and others flanked Butch on both sides.

Trump, in the United States, said Monday he thought it was вАЬa disgrace.вАЭ

вАЬI'm very open-minded,вАЭ the former president and current Republican nominee told Fox News host Laura Ingraham, who specifically asked about comparisons to вАЬThe Last Supper,вАЭ вАЬbut I thought what they did was a disgrace.вАЭ

French Catholic bishops and others were among those who said Christians had been hurt and offended.Paris Olympics organizers have saidthere was вАЬnever an intention to show disrespect to any religious groupвАЭ and that the intent was to вАЬcelebrate community tolerance.вАЭ

Jolly has said he saw the moment as a celebration of diversity, and the table on which Butch spun her tunes as a tribute to feasting and French gastronomy.

вАЬMy wish isnвАЩt to be subversive, nor to mock or to shock,вАЭ Jolly said. вАЬMost of all, I wanted to send a message of love, a message of inclusion and not at all to divide.вАЭ

Performer Philippe Katerine,who appeared in the next scenepainted blue and nearly nude in a tribute to Dionysus, also told Le Monde newspaper that вАЬThe Last SupperвАЭ had not been referenced at all in preparations for the overall sketch.

In a statement of her own, posted on Instagram, Butch said: вАЬWhatever some may say, I exist. IвАЩve never been ashamed of who I am, and I take responsibility for everything - including my artistic choices. All my life, IвАЩve refused to be a victim: I wonвАЩt shut up.вАЭ

She said she вАЬwas extremely honoredвАЭ to perform in FridayвАЩs ceremony and вАЬmy heart is still full of joy.вАЭ

вАЬIвАЩm committed, and IвАЩm proud. Proud of who I am, of what I am, and of what I embody, both for my loved ones and for millions of French people. My France is France !вАЭ she wrote.

In an AP interview Tuesday, Msellati described Butch as in вАЬa fighting spiritвАЭ вАФ eager to defend herself and her choices, and still very proud of her participation. вАЬShe has no regrets, even now,вАЭ the lawyer said.

She said hateful messages targeting Butch are вАЬarriving almost every minute,вАЭ and that Jolly and the ceremony's drag artists have also been targeted by cyberbullying.

Another performer in the controversial scene, drag queen Paloma, said Tuesday that she had not filed her own complaint.

But, she said, вАЬif the insults continue, I will join my friend Barbara Butch in her approach. For now, I am trying to focus on the on the thousands of loving messages I receive."

Of the criticism, Paloma said her вАЬfirst reaction is to say that if Donald Trump is not reacting, then we have not done our job. Unfortunately, we were going to get a negative reaction no matter what we did.вАЭ She also said it was hypocritical for critics to use religion as a basis for вАЬa reaction that is very homophobic, very transphobic, queer-phobic, drag-phobic, even antisemitic and fatphobic.вАЭ

And another drag queen in the scene, Piche, said she was вАЬreally happy that queer people were able to be represented in this show. There was no moment that the idea of offending someone or a religion was on somebody's mind. It was just a joyful, happy pop culture moment that most of the planet felt.вАЭ

___

AP journalists Nicolas Vaux-Montagny and Kwiyeon Ha contributed reporting.

___

For more coverage of the Paris Olympics, visithttps://apnews.com/hub/2024-paris-olympic-games.

title: French police investigating abuse targeting Olympic opening ceremony DJ over 'Last Supper' tableau
author: Associated Press
description: A storm of outrage about the Paris OlympicsвАЩ opening ceremony has taken a legal turn.
